title: Build Development Department Paperclip Plugin Phase 1 All Files
job:
Build the Development Department Paperclip plugin Phase 1. Create all files in plugins/dev-department/ directory.

You MUST create these files and commit them. Do not just plan or analyze — write the actual code.

1. Create plugins/dev-department/package.json with name dev-department version 0.1.0 and typescript react dependencies

2. Create plugins/dev-department/paperclip-plugin.json:
{
  "id": "dev-department",
  "apiVersion": 1,
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "displayName": "Development Department",
  "description": "Phase-aware build governance for phased architecture projects",
  "author": "Mike Jackson",
  "categories": ["productivity"],
  "capabilities": ["plugin.state.read", "plugin.state.write"],
  "entrypoints": {"worker": "src/worker/index.ts", "ui": "src/ui/index.tsx"},
  "ui": {"slots": [{"type": "projectSidebarItem", "id": "dev-dept-sidebar", "displayName": "Development Department", "exportName": "DepartmentSidebar", "entityTypes": ["project"]}, {"type": "detailTab", "id": "dev-dept-phases", "displayName": "Phases", "exportName": "PhasesTab", "entityTypes": ["project"]}]}
}

3. Create plugins/dev-department/tsconfig.json for strict TypeScript

4. Create plugins/dev-department/src/worker/types.ts with TypeScript interfaces:
- DevProject: id, name, objective, owner, status (Draft|Active|Blocked|Archived), activePhaseId, roadmapSummary, createdAt, updatedAt
- Phase: id, projectId, phaseNumber, title, objective, description, status (DraftSpec|SpecApproved|PRDAttached|ReadyForBuild|Accepted|ReworkRequired|Closed), prerequisites, successCriteria, riskNotes, freezeState (Locked|FrozenDownstream|EditableDownstream|DownstreamRevisionRequired), sortOrder, createdAt, updatedAt
- Spec: id, phaseId, title, sourceRef, version, author, approvalState, notes
- PRD: id, phaseId, title, sourceRef, version, approvalState, deviationNotes, notes
- BuildDispatch: id, phaseId, status, buildClass, riskLevel, targetRepo, environment, createdAt
- BuildOutput: id, phaseId, status, implementationSummary, artifactLinks, notes
- Review: id, phaseId, decision, comments, impactOnFuturePhases, decidedAt
- RevisionEvent: id, sourcePhaseId, affectedPhaseIds, summary, reason, approvedBy, createdAt
- ConversationReference: id, scopeType, scopeId, system, role, url, status, authoritative, notes

5. Create plugins/dev-department/src/worker/state.ts with CRUD functions for all entities using a simple in-memory store with JSON serialization

6. Create plugins/dev-department/src/worker/index.ts exporting all state operations

7. Create plugins/dev-department/src/ui/index.tsx as React UI entrypoint

8. Create React components in plugins/dev-department/src/ui/components/:
- DepartmentView.tsx - main view showing project list or detail
- ProjectHeader.tsx - project name, objective, status dropdown
- PhaseList.tsx - ordered phase list with status badges and reorder
- PhaseDetail.tsx - edit phase fields, linked spec/PRD, conversation refs
- ConversationRefs.tsx - add/edit conversation URLs with system and role dropdowns

Freeze state and phase status are INDEPENDENT. Phase ordering uses sortOrder. No PopeBot dependency. TypeScript throughout.
